channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Fighting assault allegations, Eric Swalwell suspends his bid for California governor
item.description: The San Francisco Chronicle and CNN reported multiple allegations of sexual assault and misconduct. Swalwell calls them false but apologized to his supporters and family.
item.content:encoded:
The San Francisco Chronicle and CNN reported multiple allegations of sexual assault and misconduct. Swalwell calls them false but apologized to his supporters and family.

(Image credit: Rich Pedroncelli)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Will Orbán's defeat change Hungary's relationship to the EU?
item.description: Historic voter turnout in Hungary ousted Prime Minister Viktor Orbán from power. This shift may dramatically change Hungary's relationship with the European Union, which Orbán criticized regularly.
item.content:encoded: Historic voter turnout in Hungary ousted Prime Minister Viktor Orbán from power. This shift may dramatically change Hungary's relationship with the European Union, which Orbán criticized regularly.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in historic Hungary election
item.description: Hungarians turned out in historic numbers to vote against Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's and his Fidesz party. NPR's Rob Schmitz discusses with reporter Esme Nicholson and political scientist Abel Bojar.
item.content:encoded: Hungarians turned out in historic numbers to vote against Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's and his Fidesz party. NPR's Rob Schmitz discusses with reporter Esme Nicholson and political scientist Abel Bojar.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: The consequences of Hungary's election, for Viktor Orban and the world
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with political scientist Abel Bojar about the results of Hungary's election, the far-reaching consequences of the vote and the future for Viktor Orban.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with political scientist Abel Bojar about the results of Hungary's election, the far-reaching consequences of the vote and the future for Viktor Orban.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Hungary's Viktor Orbán concedes defeat, ending 16 years in power
item.description: Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party.
item.content:encoded:
Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party.

(Image credit: Attila Kisbenedek)
item.dc:creator: Esme Nicholson

item.title: A country-by-country glance at Pope Leo XIV's trip to Africa
item.description: Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his early years.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV's four-nation, 11-day trip to Africa is so dizzying in its complexity it recalls some of the globetrotting odysseys of St. John Paul II in his early years.

(Image credit: Andrew Medichini)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A Hezbollah commander describes battling Israel in Lebanon
item.description: In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.
item.content:encoded:
In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: The U.S. military says it will blockade Iranian ports as Iran peace talks collapse
item.description: After talks between the U.S. and Iran collapsed, President Trump said the U.S. will "blockade" the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military said it would blockade ships entering or exiting Iranian ports.
item.content:encoded:
After talks between the U.S. and Iran collapsed, President Trump said the U.S. will "blockade" the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military said it would blockade ships entering or exiting Iranian ports.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
item.description: The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.
item.content:encoded:
The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.

(Image credit: Jonathan Olley)
item.dc:creator: Tara Haelle

item.title: White House ballroom construction can continue for now, appeals court says
item.description: The order comes as the Trump administration challenges a lower court ruling that the estimated $300-million project requires congressional approval.
item.content:encoded:
The order comes as the Trump administration challenges a lower court ruling that the estimated $300-million project requires congressional approval.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster
